stories.title: Thunder 108, Lakers 90 — Holmgren 24/12/3 Carries OKC to Game 1 Win

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Chet Holmgren: 24 PTS, 12 REB, 3 BLK.

OKC without Jalen Williams. Lakers without Luka Doncic.

Thunder still win by 18. They are 5-and-0 in the 2026 playoffs. The depth on this team is something else. #ThunderUp #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
SGA had 18 points last night. That's considered a quiet night for him.

Ajay Mitchell added 18. Holmgren went for 24/12/3.

Thunder won by 18. LeBron scored 27 and it wasn't close. Oklahoma City is the class of the West. #ThunderUp #LakeShow #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Oklahoma City had an "off night" from Shai Gilgeous-Alexander — just 18 points by his standards — and they still won by 18.

That's how deep this Thunder team is.

Chet Holmgren was the story in Game 1 against the Lakers on Tuesday: 24 points on 9-of-17 shooting, 12 rebounds, and 3 blocks in 31 minutes. He was dominant at both ends, giving OKC a weapon to lean on when SGA's output wasn't at his usual MVP level.

Ajay Mitchell chipped in 18 points as well, proving once again that this roster goes 10 deep.

For the Lakers, LeBron James did everything in his power — 27 points on 12-of-17 from the field, 6 assists — but without Luka Doncic (Grade 2 hamstring, out since April 2), there simply wasn't enough firepower around him.

The Thunder are now 5-0 in the 2026 playoffs, and they haven't had Jalen Williams in the lineup for three straight games.

Oklahoma City is the class of the Western Conference. Period.
facebook.image_caption: OKC wins Game 1 without two All-Stars in the lineup. Holmgren: 24 PTS, 12 REB, 3 BLK. The Thunder depth machine is something else. #ThunderUp #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Pistons 111, Cavaliers 101 — Cade 23/7, Harris 20/8 Power Detroit to Series Lead

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Cade: 23 pts, 7 ast. Harris: 20 pts, 8 reb.

Detroit 111, Cleveland 101 in Game 1. Pistons snap a 12-game playoff losing streak against the Cavs — and earn their first Game 1 playoff win since 2008.

This team keeps making history. #DetroitBasketball #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Detroit Pistons aren't done making history.

On Tuesday, they beat the Cleveland Cavaliers 111-101 in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Semifinals — snapping a 12-game playoff losing streak against the Cavs and earning their first Game 1 playoff win since May 2008 against the Orlando Magic.

Cade Cunningham was sharp: 23 points and 7 assists. Tobias Harris continued his exceptional playoff run with 20 points and 8 rebounds. The Pistons' balanced attack made it incredibly difficult for Cleveland to key in on any single scorer.

The Cavaliers had their moments — Donovan Mitchell scored 23 and James Harden poured in 22, including 13 in the fourth quarter. But Detroit's depth and home-court energy proved to be too much.

This Pistons team keeps finding ways to win. They came back from 3-1 against Milwaukee. They beat Orlando in Game 7. And now they're controlling the Cavaliers from the opening tip.

Game 2 is Thursday, May 7, at 7:00 PM ET on Prime Video at Little Caesars Arena.
facebook.image_caption: Pistons 111, Cavs 101. Cade: 23 pts. Harris: 20 pts. Detroit ends a 12-game playoff skid against Cleveland and grabs the Game 1 advantage. #DetroitBasketball #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Luka Watch — Return Could Come as Early as May 7, Game 4 (May 11) More Realistic

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Luka update: ESPN listed May 7 as a possible return. Sources closer to camp point to Game 4 (May 11) as more realistic.

He hasn't started full-speed running yet. Out since April 2. Lakers trail 0-1. #LakeShow #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The most important injury update in the NBA right now: where is Luka Doncic?

The Lakers star has been sidelined since April 2 with a Grade 2 left hamstring strain — that's 33-plus days out. ESPN recently updated their return target to as early as May 7, which sent some hope through Laker Nation. But sources closer to the situation point to Game 4 (May 11) at Crypto.com Arena as the more realistic target.

The reason for caution is simple: Doncic still hasn't begun full-speed running or one-on-one contact drills. That's the gap that takes time to close safely — especially with a Grade 2 strain, where rushing the process risks re-injury.

In the meantime, LeBron James dropped 27 points on 12-of-17 shooting in Game 1's 90-108 loss — heroic, but not enough against a Thunder team that is 5-0 in these playoffs and won by 18 without their own All-Star in the lineup.

Every game Luka misses is a game the Lakers may not get back.
facebook.image_caption: Luka update: ESPN says May 7 possible. Sources near camp point to Game 4 (May 11). Lakers trail 0-1. The clock is ticking. #LakeShow #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Can Embiid Bounce Back? Knicks vs. 76ers Game 2 Tonight at MSG — 7:10 PM ET

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
TONIGHT at MSG: Knicks vs. 76ers, Game 2. 7:10 PM ET, ESPN.

New York has won 4 straight playoff games by 16, 29, 51 and 39 points. Embiid shot 3-for-11 in Game 1.

Can Philly find a way? Or does the Knicks express keep rolling? #NewYorkForever #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Tonight at Madison Square Garden, the New York Knicks have a chance to go up 2-0 against the Philadelphia 76ers. And they have every reason to believe they can do it again.

In Game 1, New York won 137-98. Jalen Brunson scored 35 — 27 in the first half. The Knicks shot 63 percent from the field and led by 40 at one point. It was a complete obliteration.

For Philadelphia to respond, Joel Embiid simply must be better. He shot 3-for-11 in Game 1 for just 14 points — far below what the Sixers need. The 76ers also turned the ball over 19 times and shot 41 percent from the field.

The Knicks have now won four straight playoff games by margins of 16, 29, 51, and 39 points. They are the most dominant team in the Eastern Conference right now, and they're doing it with elite defense and Brunson playing at the peak of his career.

Can Philly stop the bleeding? Game 2 tips off tonight at 7:10 PM ET on ESPN.
facebook.image_caption: The Knicks are on a historic roll. Can the 76ers bounce back in Game 2 at MSG? 7:10 PM ET, ESPN. #NewYorkForever #NBAPlayoffs

stories.title: Wolves vs. Spurs Game 2 Tonight — San Antonio Must Slow Edwards, 9:30 PM ET

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Wembanyama had 12 BLOCKS in Game 1. An all-time NBA playoff record.

The Spurs still lost by 2.

Game 2 tonight — Wolves vs. Spurs, 9:30 PM ET, ESPN. San Antonio needs answers for Anthony Edwards. #GoSpursGo #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Victor Wembanyama blocked 12 shots in Game 1. That's an all-time NBA playoff record — shattering the previous mark of 10. The Spurs still lost by two.

That tells you everything you need to know about the challenge San Antonio faces tonight.

Anthony Edwards — who came back from a hyperextended knee 10 days ahead of schedule — scored 18 points off the bench in the Timberwolves' 104-102 Game 1 victory, including 11 in the fourth quarter. His clutch plays were the difference.

Now the series shifts to San Antonio for Game 2 tonight at 9:30 PM ET on ESPN. The Spurs face a critical question: how do you stop Anthony Edwards? He looked fully healthy, fully engaged, and fully dangerous in Game 1.

Wembanyama's historic defensive performance showed the world his ceiling. But his team needs more offensive production to capitalize on it.
facebook.image_caption: Wemby had 12 blocks — an all-time playoff record. Spurs still lost. Game 2 tonight at 9:30 PM ET on ESPN. Can San Antonio find offensive answers? #GoSpursGo #NBAPlayoffs
